mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, tight labor market conditions signal accelerating wage growth and robust consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window as earnings expectations upgrade.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the signal, or the signal fails to predict returns when UNRATE crosses below 4.0 in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in residential construction starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates synchronized housing and consumption demand expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate revenue growth accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals capacity utilization improvement during early-cycle recoveries, leading to positive 5-day forward equity returns as manufacturing earnings revisions turn positive and supply chain bottlenecks ease.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, indicating that supply growth is outpacing demand and compressing margins.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation above a 0.4 percent monthly pace forces aggressive Federal Reserve tightening expectations, compressing equity valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite inflation exceeding the threshold, or forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates above the trigger level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic momentum, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day window as corporate earnings outlook improves across cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of rolling 3-year windows, or the cross-sectional correlation between the proxies and forward returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as analyst revisions cascade through equity prices.
failure_condition: Walk-forward information coefficient remains below 0.02 over any 3-year period, or GDP growth quarters consistently produce negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid unemployment deterioration alongside slowing payroll growth signals an impending earnings recession, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as risk aversion spikes and discount rates rise.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive when the trigger activates, or the joint deterioration of labor indicators fails to precede equity drawdowns in more than 40% of historical instances.
